steps.title: Identify the “with” prepositional phrase structure
steps.content: The blank appears within “with the Vercelli map... another one”—a “with” prepositional phrase where a noun needs to connect to a predicate nominative.

steps.title: Apply the participle rule for “with” constructions
steps.content:
In a “with” prepositional phrase, a participle (the “-ing” form) is required to connect the noun to the predicate, not a finite verb. “Being” creates the correct form: “with the Vercelli map being another one.” Finite verbs like was, is, and has been would create awkward independent clauses.

That matches Choice B — being.
